revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:秋殿南古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Akidono Minami Kofun | image = File:Akidono Minami Kofun, funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = Akidono Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.500305555555556|N|135.85744444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| iso = JP-29 | type = [[Square tumulus|Square]] | area = 26 meters on each side | burial = {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543435}} | burial goods = | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} (or early 7th century) | Interred_individual = | Historic Site = None | notable_features = | map = Japan Nara | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | map_dot_label = Akidono Minami Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Akidono Minami Kofun''' (秋殿南古墳, Akidono Kofun) is a square-shaped tumulus located in [[Asago, Sakurai City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is not Historic Site1 as a cultural or historical site. == Overview == Situated in the southern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, Akidono Minami Kofun was built into the southern edge of a ridge extending southward from Mount Torimi. While the mound has been surveyed, no excavations have been conducted{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. The tumulus is square-shaped (truncated pyramidal type) and measures approximately 26 meters on each side{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. No evidence of surface features such as [[Fukiishi]] or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} has been detected{{Sfn|Akidono Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543435}} opening southward{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. The total length of the chamber is 11.2 meters (up to the ceiling stone) or 13.7 meters (including the estimated extension of the passage walls), making it a large stone chamber constructed from massive {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} boulders{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. The tumulus dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}} (or the very beginning of the 7th century{{Sfn|Akidono Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}). The Akidono North Kofun is located on the same ridge, but the tomb groups within the ridges of Mount Torimi exhibit distinct characteristics, making this an important site for studying burial zones{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. == burial == [[File:Akidono Minami Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Akidono Minami Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded diagram of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11543435}} opening to the south. Its area are as follows{{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}: * Total length: 11.2 meters (to the tip of the ceiling stone) or 13.7 meters (to the estimated end of the passage walls) * burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 4.48 meters ** Width: 2.26 meters ** Height: 2.4 meters * Entrance corridor (羨道): ** Width: 1.7 meters The chamber walls are constructed from roughly hewn granite blocks. The burial chamber walls are two tiers high, while the entrance corridor walls are one tier (4 blocks on the west side and 3 on the east side){{Sfn|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}. The ceiling consists of two stones in the burial chamber and four stones in the corridor. The chamber exhibits similarities to the [[Iwayayama Kofun]] (located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}) and is categorized as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber," though it is considered slightly earlier in construction{{Sfn|Akidono Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. It also shares design elements with the [[Tsukamoto Kofun]] and [[Uchiage Kofun]] (both in Asuka)<ref>[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0008.html Akidono Minami Kofun] (Nara Prefecture Website "2010 Scenery of Nara").</ref>. <gallery> Iwayayama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Stone chamber of [[Iwayayama Kofun]] Akidono Minami Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|burial chamber (towards the back wall) Akidono Minami Kofun, genshitsu-2-3.jpg|burial chamber (towards the entrance) Akidono Minami Kofun, sendou-2-3.jpg|Entrance corridor (towards the entrance) Akidono Minami Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (towards the burial chamber) Akidono Minami Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance </gallery> == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Interpretive board installed by Sakurai City Board of Education * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Akidono Kofun|title=Place names of Nara Prefecture|series=[[Historical Place names of Japan]] Vol. 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Akidono Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=Akidono Minami Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Akidono Minami Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|chapter=Akidono Minami Kofun|year=2010|title=Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|publisher=Sakurai City Historic Site Association|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Exploring the Horizontal Stone Chambers of Sakurai|2010|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Akidono Minami Kofun|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
